verses.text: He that climbs in another way is a thief; the shepherd enters by the door, and the sheep hear his voice. True and false. The flock knows its rightful shepherd.
He that climbs in another way is a thief; the shepherd enters by the door, and the sheep hear his voice. True and false. The flock knows its rightful shepherd.
He that climbs in another way is a thief; the shepherd enters by the door, and the sheep hear his voice. True and false. The flock knows its rightful shepherd.
He goes before them, and they follow, knowing his voice; a stranger they flee. Following the voice. Christ's own know and heed Him.
He goes before them, and they follow, knowing his voice; a stranger they flee. Following the voice. Christ's own know and heed Him.
He goes before them, and they follow, knowing his voice; a stranger they flee. Following the voice. Christ's own know and heed Him.
'I am the door': by Me if any enter in, 'he shall be saved,' and find pasture. The only way. Christ is the entrance to safety and life.
'I am the door': by Me if any enter in, 'he shall be saved,' and find pasture. The only way. Christ is the entrance to safety and life.
'I am the door': by Me if any enter in, 'he shall be saved,' and find pasture. The only way. Christ is the entrance to safety and life.
'I am come that they might have life'; 'I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.' Sacrificial love. He lays down His life for His flock.
'I am come that they might have life'; 'I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.' Sacrificial love. He lays down His life for His flock.
The hireling flees when the wolf comes, caring not for the sheep. False shepherds. The mercenary deserts the flock in danger.
The hireling flees when the wolf comes, caring not for the sheep. False shepherds. The mercenary deserts the flock in danger.
'I know my sheep, and am known of mine'; other sheep I have; 'one fold, and one shepherd.' One flock. Jew and Gentile are gathered under one Lord.
'I know my sheep, and am known of mine'; other sheep I have; 'one fold, and one shepherd.' One flock. Jew and Gentile are gathered under one Lord.
'I know my sheep, and am known of mine'; other sheep I have; 'one fold, and one shepherd.' One flock. Jew and Gentile are gathered under one Lord.
'I lay down my life, that I might take it again'; no man takes it from Me. Willing death. He gives His life freely and takes it again.
'I lay down my life, that I might take it again'; no man takes it from Me. Willing death. He gives His life freely and takes it again.
There is a division; some say He has a devil, others, can a devil open blind eyes? Divided again. His works confound the slander against Him.
There is a division; some say He has a devil, others, can a devil open blind eyes? Divided again. His works confound the slander against Him.
There is a division; some say He has a devil, others, can a devil open blind eyes? Divided again. His works confound the slander against Him.
At the feast they ask, 'If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.' Demand for proof. They ask, yet are unwilling to believe.
At the feast they ask, 'If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.' Demand for proof. They ask, yet are unwilling to believe.
At the feast they ask, 'If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.' Demand for proof. They ask, yet are unwilling to believe.
I told you, and ye believed not; 'ye are not of my sheep.' Unbelief explained. They do not hear because they are not His.
I told you, and ye believed not; 'ye are not of my sheep.' Unbelief explained. They do not hear because they are not His.
'My sheep hear my voice... I give unto them eternal life... neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.' Eternal security. Christ's own are safe in His grip.
'My sheep hear my voice... I give unto them eternal life... neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.' Eternal security. Christ's own are safe in His grip.
My Father is greater than all; 'I and my Father are one.' Oneness with God. Christ claims full equality with the Father.
My Father is greater than all; 'I and my Father are one.' Oneness with God. Christ claims full equality with the Father.
They take up stones; for which work? 'for blasphemy... thou... makest thyself God.' Plain claim heard. They understood He claimed deity.
They take up stones; for which work? 'for blasphemy... thou... makest thyself God.' Plain claim heard. They understood He claimed deity.
They take up stones; for which work? 'for blasphemy... thou... makest thyself God.' Plain claim heard. They understood He claimed deity.
If I do the Father's works, believe the works, 'that ye may know... the Father is in me, and I in him.' Works as witness. His deeds prove His union with God.
If I do the Father's works, believe the works, 'that ye may know... the Father is in me, and I in him.' Works as witness. His deeds prove His union with God.
If I do the Father's works, believe the works, 'that ye may know... the Father is in me, and I in him.' Works as witness. His deeds prove His union with God.
If I do the Father's works, believe the works, 'that ye may know... the Father is in me, and I in him.' Works as witness. His deeds prove His union with God.
If I do the Father's works, believe the works, 'that ye may know... the Father is in me, and I in him.' Works as witness. His deeds prove His union with God.
They seek to take Him, but He escapes; beyond Jordan many believe. Fruit elsewhere. Where He is rejected, others receive Him.
They seek to take Him, but He escapes; beyond Jordan many believe. Fruit elsewhere. Where He is rejected, others receive Him.
They seek to take Him, but He escapes; beyond Jordan many believe. Fruit elsewhere. Where He is rejected, others receive Him.
